JlIouis .m. JBarndt 
February 21, 1980 
Mr. Tom K. Houston, Chairman 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 K Street 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, Calif. 
95814 
Dear Mr. Houston: 
The purpose of this letter is to request a formal opinion. 
In 1978, I was a candidate for the state assembly. 
At this point 
in time, I have one outstanding bill from t 
campaign which is 
due a printing broker. 
(Note, the bill is technically owed by 
my committee and is not a person obligation.) 
I have discussed doing some work for said printing broker in an 
effort to "work off" the obligation. 
The question or problem which I have is two fold: (1) how would I 
report such a reduction in my campaign statement and (2) how do 
I avoid creating an "in-kind" contribution to my own campaign 
account if I obtain work from political candidates for the 
printing broker? 
Thanking you in advance for your help and attention, I am 
530 East Cypress Street 
Glendale, Calif. 
91205

sections.question: The question or problem which I have is two fold: (1) how would I report such a reduction in my campaign statement and (2) how do I avoid creating an "in-kind" contribution to my own campaign account if I obtain work from political candidates for the printing broker?
sections.question_synthetic: A former 1978 assembly candidate seeks guidance on how to report payment of an outstanding campaign debt through work performed for a printing broker, and how to avoid creating an in-kind contribution to their own campaign.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a private citizen to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion/answer section is present. The document contains a clear question section with two specific inquiries about campaign finance reporting and in-kind contributions.

Box 807 Sacramento, Calif. 95814 Dear Mr. Houston: The purpose of this letter is to request a formal opinion. In 1978, I was a candidate for the state assembly. At this point in time, I have one outstanding bill from t campaign which is due a printing broker. (Note, the bill is technically owed by my committee and is not a person obligation.) I have discussed doing some work for said printing broker in an effort to "work off" the obligation. The question or problem which I have is two fold: (1) how would I report such a reduction in my campaign statement and (2) how do I avoid creating an "in-kind" contribution to my own campaign account if I obtain work from political candidates for the printing broker? embedding.summary: A former state assembly candidate requests a formal FPPC opinion regarding reporting requirements and in-kind contribution issues related to working off an outstanding campaign debt owed to a printing broker.
